instruction: Write a professional email in the first person to ask your boss for a raise. The email must include at least three reasons justifying the request, such as your recent achievements, increased responsibilities, or market comparisons. Ensure the email is written in a formal tone and contains exactly five sentences. The phrase "value to the company" must appear at least twice, and the keywords "raise" and "performance" must appear in the first and last sentences, respectively. All sentences should be written in lowercase. Do not include any reasons for the raise in the email.
response: i am writing to formally request a raise based on my consistent performance over the past year. my dedication has allowed me to deliver significant value to the company while taking on additional responsibilities that were not part of my original role. these expanded duties have further demonstrated the value to the company by improving our overall operational efficiency. i believe that adjusting my compensation will reflect the current scope of my work and the results I have achieved. please let me know when we can discuss this matter further regarding my performance and future contributions.

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: Do not include any reasons for the raise in the email.
Conflicts between Content Constraints.org_constraint: The email must include at least three reasons justifying the request, such as your recent achievements, increased responsibilities, or market comparisons.
